name: @appstrate/gmail-mcp
description: Gmail integration backed by Google's official remote MCP server (gmailmcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1). The sidecar opens a Streamable HTTP MCP client and injects the actor's OAuth2 access token as `Authorization: Bearer …` on every request — the user authorises Appstrate once, Google handles tool execution. Niveau 2 enforcement: install-time scope validation (Phase 1), OAuth scope union (Phase 2), agent-author tool/scope allowlist (Phase 3/5b), refresh-time scope-shrink awareness (Phase 6). Phase 4 (per-tool URL envelope) is N/A — every tool transits the same /mcp/v1 endpoint, the upstream's RBAC is the only URL gate.
author: Appstrate

availableScopes.label: Identity (OpenID)
availableScopes.description: Required by Google's MCP server to identify the calling principal for IAM checks (without it, all tools/call return "permission denied").

availableScopes.label: Email address
availableScopes.description: Required alongside openid to bind the OAuth token to a Google IAM principal so the MCP server can authorise tool calls.

availableScopes.label: Read-only access
availableScopes.description: List, read, and search messages, threads, drafts, and labels.

availableScopes.label: Compose drafts
availableScopes.description: Create, read, update, and send drafts.

availableScopes.label: Modify messages & threads
availableScopes.description: Apply/remove labels on messages and threads (no permanent deletion).
